mechanism_narrative: A rise in the unemployment rate above 7.5% triggers a contractionary monetary policy response, which reduces labor demand and subsequently leads to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains above 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 60-day forward returns in contractionary periods are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A decline in the housing market, as indicated by a drop in the Houston shipbuilding index, signals a weakening demand for goods and services, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains above 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 5-day forward returns in weakening-demand periods are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: An increase in the industrial production index above 100 signals a strengthening supply of goods and services, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains above 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in strengthening-supply periods are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A decline in the consumer price index below 100 signals a weakening inflationary pressure, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains above 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 60-day forward returns in weakening-inflation periods are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A rise in the payroll employment index above 150,000 signals a strengthening labor market, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains above 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in strengthening-labor periods are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A decline in the unemployment rate below 4% signals a strengthening labor market, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains above 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 60-day forward returns in strengthening-labor periods are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A decline in the retail sales index below -1% signals a weakening demand for goods and services, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains above 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 5-day forward returns in weakening-demand periods are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: An increase in the industrial production index above 110 signals a strengthening supply of goods and services, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains above 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in strengthening-supply periods are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).
